error.signature: Changes to launch files or config not reflected after rebuild with colcon --symlink-install

verdict.summary: --symlink-install creates symlinks for Python files and launch files but NOT for compiled C++ artifacts or generated files. Also, newly added files require a full rebuild to create the symlink. Deleted files leave stale symlinks.

dead_ends.action: Use --symlink-install and expect all file changes to be reflected automatically
dead_ends.why_fails: Only existing Python/launch files are symlinked. New files, CMakeLists.txt changes, and generated files require colcon build to update.

dead_ends.action: Delete install/ directory and rebuild to fix stale symlinks
dead_ends.why_fails: Works but is slow. You also need to delete build/ for some cases. A targeted rebuild of the affected package is faster.

workarounds.action: Run 'colcon build --packages-select <pkg>' after adding new files
workarounds.how: colcon build --symlink-install --packages-select my_package  # rebuilds only the affected package
source install/setup.bash  # re-source after rebuild to pick up new packages and files

workarounds.action: For C++ changes, always run colcon build (symlink-install doesn't help for compiled code)
